text.title: Idea Generation for Solopreneurs Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Idea Generation for Solopreneurs application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Session seeds with explicit constraints (hours/week, budget, skills, segment). 'Find me a billion-dollar idea' style open prompts are rejected.
rule.rationale: Specific constraints surface opportunities the open prompt never reaches.

rule.statement: Generate ≥30 candidates across the seven frameworks BEFORE scoring any. Mid-session scoring is rejected.
rule.rationale: Early scoring kills divergent options; convergence works only on a wide enough pool.

rule.statement: Scoring runs in a second agent context, not the generation context. Same-context scoring rationalises its own output.
rule.rationale: Agents defend their own generations; separation forces honest evaluation.

rule.statement: Personal fit <3 overrides any weighted total — reject the idea regardless of market score.
rule.rationale: Fit determines whether the operator will ship; market alone does not.

rule.statement: Score at most 5 candidates rigorously. Scoring 30 is theatre; pre-filter to top-5 by gut, then score.
rule.rationale: Excessive scoring dilutes attention; 5 candidates fit working memory and review.

rule.statement: Deduplicate candidates by (audience × problem) pair, not by name. Market-stacking + unbundling often produce the same idea twice.
rule.rationale: Name-based dedup leaves duplicate audience-problem pairs that inflate the pool.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
